rulings.Case Title: RAY CATUDAL VS. RANDALL FILLMORE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 15, 2011, line 3. DEFENDANT MONTE STOTT & ASSOCIATES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. No expert declaration or evidence presented regarding the professional standard of care. Triable issues of fact raised whether defendants nonfeasance (breach) was a substantial factor in causing injury. Experts Panasci and Hallet declare that defendant had a duty to provide complete plans that included providing sufficient detail regarding the railings. Mr. Paul stated that Mr. Stott admitted the plans should have more detail regarding the guard rail (depo pp. 105-106). Whether defendant's nonfeasance/defective plans were a substantial factor in causing injury presents a factual issue. It was foreseeable that unprofessional contractors may fill the gaps in the plans in an unsafe manner. Negligence per se is not a cause of action, but an evidentiary presumption that cannot be disposed of under CCP 437 c(f)(1). Although defendant did not own, lease, occupy, or maintain the property, no evidence is presented that defendant cannot be held liable for premises liability by control of the property or the creation of a dangerous condition. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN WHITEHOUSE VS. PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 15, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied. 2nd cause of action (reasonable accommodations) triable issues of fact raised as to whether the accommodations offered were reasonable, whether other accommodations should have been considered, and whether plaintiff was capable of performing the job of a communications technician with an accommodation. (Plaintiff's response to defendants UMFs 4-6, 10-12, 15-16, 30, 71 and 79). 3rd cause of action (failure to engage in interactive process) triable issues of fact raised whether a reasonable accommodation was possible or could have been provided but was never explored. (Same UMFs). 1st cause of action (disability discrimination) triable issues of fact raised whether plaintiff was reasonably accommodated and whether plaintiff could have performed the job related function of the communications technician position with an accommodation (same UMFs). 5th cause of action (wrongful termination in violation of public policy) is derivitive and dependent upon plaintiff's FEHA discrimination claim. 4th cause of action (retaliation) is reasonably related to the claims that were raised in the DFEH complaint. Defendant does not shift the burden and cites no evidence that plaintiff did not engage in a protected act: plaintiff raises a triable issue of fact that she complained about the forced leave of absence (response to UMF 37). Defendant does not shift the burden regarding punitive damages. Defendant presents no discovery admission or other admissible evidence showing that plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence. Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co (2001) 25 Cal 4th 826, 854; Gaggero v. Yura (2003) 108 CA 4th 884, 891. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE TAYLOR et al VS. AURORA LOAN SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings , Points And Authoritiesand Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 15, 2011, line 10. DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICES LLC Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Granted with leave to amend. All four causes of action are premised on the allegation that defendant had a contractual obligation to grant plaintiffs loan modification if plaintiffs made payments under the forebearance agreement. The clear language of the agreement provides that there was nothing automatic in the granting of a modification. Plaintiffs allege a breach by the refusal to grant without specifying any such language in the agreement. The covenant of good faith and fair dealing cannot impose substantive duties on the contracting parties beyond those incorporated in the specific terms of the agreement. The UCL claim cannot be maintained in the absence of any other law successfully alleged to have been violated by defendant. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMIE HERNANDEZ et al VS. MARTIN RESENDEZ GUAJARDO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 15, 2011, line 11. PLAINTIFF JAMIE HERNANDEZ, JAGDEEP SINGH, GUILLERMO ORTEGA DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled as to the 7th, 8th, 9th, 11th, 16th and 17th affirmative defenses. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 5th, 10th and 13th affirmative defenses. For unclean hands defendant must allege a wrong independent from allegedly filing a lawsuit. Intervening cause and consent must be pled with greater specificity. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the 6th, 12th, 14th and 15th affirmative defenses that are inapplicable to this action. Motion to strike granted without leave to amend as to last sentence of paragraphs 50 and 57 and paragraphs 78 and 82 that are irreleveant. Remainder is moot. Plaintiffs to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 (c). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., AS TRUSTEE VS. SHEILA VINSON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 15, 2011, line 14. DEFENDANTS MARGARITA PEREZ, PATRICIA GARCIA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 5 days leave to amend. Complaint contradictorily alleges that defendants are not tenants, but breached the lease. Complaint does not sufficiently allege how defendants violated a lawful obligation of the tenancy per SFAC 37.9 (a)(2). =(302/CWW)
